posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I want to be able to specify a minimum and/or maximum value that a column can have.

What are you trying to achieve

In my specific case, I am using a numeric column to store a year, and I want to limit entries into that column to years between 1900 and the current year. But I could envision that this feature would be valuable in any number of scenarios.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
This actually exists now

You can use Data Validations to set rules like minimum and maximum values on number columns, which should cover your year use case (e.g. 1900 to current year).

It lets you:

  • define allowed ranges

  • prevent invalid entries at the source

  • guide users with clear validation messages

So instead of relying on workarounds or fixing data later, you can enforce it directly in the column.

Definitely worth checking out if you haven’t already, it solves exactly what you’re describing.

Data validations – Support

posts.author: bwinett (Bill Winett)
posts.cookedHtml: Ah, thanks. I’m working with a nonprofit currently on the free plan. When they upgrade, I’ll look at this again. Thanks.

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @bwinett
You can enforce it using Make automation

How it would work

Create a scenario that triggers whenever the Numbers column changes. Then Make will:

  • Check the entered value

  • Compare it against your allowed range (e.g., 1900 → current year)

  • If the value is outside the range, automatically take action such as:

    • Correct the value

    • Clear the field

    • Change a status to flag an error

    • Notify the user

    • Move the item to a review group
